differential_diagnoses: Iron deficiency anemia (likely gastrointestinal blood loss)
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
Underlying pulmonary disease progression (sequelae of GPA)
next_steps: Complete blood count with peripheral smear and iron studies
Measurement of serum N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP)
Repeat transthoracic echocardiography to assess hemodynamics and valve area
Fecal occult blood testing and evaluation for occult gastrointestinal malignancy
Cardiac stress testing or coronary angiography

differential_diagnoses: Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
next_steps: Obtain complete blood count and iron panel to assess for and characterize anemia
Repeat transthoracic echocardiography to assess aortic valve area and hemodynamics
Measure serum NT-proBNP and troponin levels
Perform electrocardiogram and continuous cardiac monitoring to assess for arrhythmias
Evaluate for occult gastrointestinal blood loss, including fecal immunochemical testing and consideration for endoscopy
Formal geriatric assessment for frailty and functional status to guide management goals
Review and reconcile current antithrombotic therapy (aspirin vs. anticoagulation)
Pulmonary function testing to evaluate for restrictive disease related to prior GPA
